free_text: Statistical model and cross sections for the
evaporation residues formed in the reaction
22Ne + 190-Os at the 22Ne projectile bombarding energy
of 6.0-10.0 MeV/nucleon
Beams of 22-Ne with an energy of 160, 192, and
217 MeV were used. In the experiments the beam
Intensity was Limited to 2.E+11 1/sec.
The beam energy was varied by steps of 3-6 MeV
using aluminum or titanium foils. The beam energy on
the target was determined by measuring the energy of
the ions scattered from the target at 30 degr.
Corrections for the ionization defect and effect of
the detector "dead" surface layer were not considered.
A detector array involving two wide aperture
time-of-flight (start and stop) detectors placed in
front of the Si detector of an area of 60 X 60 mm2 was
employed for the detection of evaporation residues in
the separator focal plane. The evaporation residues,
arriving at the focal plane, first passed the
time-of-flight detectors where on a base of 40 cm their
flight time was measured with a resolution of 0.5 ns.
Then, these residues were implanted into the Si
detector where their energy signals were obtained. The
Si detector recorded also the a decays of the implanted
nuclei.
The errors of the absolute ion energy
values estimated in this way made +(1.0-1.5)%
The errors of the obtained cross sections
involved statistical errors, the beam current, and
Inaccuracy in the measurements of the
separation efficiency [+/- 20%],
Uncertainty due to the target thickness [+/-5%]
The errors of the relative cross section values are
less by a factor of two or three.
A target of isotopically enriched 190-Os [280 ug/cm2]
was used in the experiments. The content of the other
Os isotopes in the target material made 1.02, 0.64, and
0.47%, respectively, for 192, 189, 188-Os. The target
was prepared by vacuum evaporation onto a thin
[36 ug/cm2] carbon foil. The free surface of the
osmium deposit was covered with a ~5 ug/cm2 carbon
layer.
HIVAP code
Tbl. 1 from Nucl.Phys.,A626(1997)857
Remarks of VML are included.
Last checking has been done.
DATE is corrected

- By Authors - Contribution from the 6N channel is not
subtracted.
